title: Char-Grill-Yang Australian Squid Skewers
description:
Savory, pillowy, one-pot Australian dish featuring squid, char-grill-yang, halal-friendly.
Perfect for a quick lunch or dinner.
instructions: Cut the squid tubes into 5cm pieces and thread onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
In a large bowl, whisk together char-grill-yang marinade, coconut oil, garlic, ginger paste, lemongrass, red onion, fish sauce, brown sugar, lime juice, salt, black pepper, and chilli flakes.
Add the squid skewers to the marinade and mix well to coat, cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat, thread the skewers onto the grill and cook for 4-6 minutes per side, or until the squid is cooked through.
While the squid is cooking, cook the pillowy rice according to package instructions.
To assemble the skewers, place a portion of cooked rice onto a serving plate, top with a squid skewer, a slice of green papaya, a slice of cucumber, and a sprinkle of fresh coriander.
Garnish with additional char-grill-yang marinade and serve immediately.
Serve with additional rice and any desired accompaniments.
Transfer the cooked squid to a plate and let rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Cook the pillowy rice according to package instructions, then fluff with a fork.
To assemble the skewers, place a portion of cooked rice onto a serving plate, top with a squid skewer, a slice of green papaya, a slice of cucumber, and a sprinkle of fresh coriander.
Transfer the cooked squid to a plate and let rest for 5 minutes before serving.
